summary: Stepping back, the core tension today is the stark divergence between a rates market preparing for a hawkish, inflation-focused Fed and an equity market still priced for an earnings-driven melt-up. This continues the theme we have tracked all week: the collision of regimes.

Stepping back, the core tension today is the widening chasm between a bond market that has declared the free lunch over and an equity market still priced for a soft landing. This continues the theme we have tracked all week: the collision of regimes. As you head into today's session, the single most important variable to monitor is the crude oil price action and its immediate pass-through to short-term inflation breakevens. If energy prices continue to climb, the rates market will keep squeezing the front end, and the pricing discrepancy between rates and credit will become unsustainable.
